title: Give judgment-light scan phases explicit fast-tier envelopes + 1-2K report budgets — the universal {balanced,deep} floor makes checklist work pay judgment prices
summary: OPERATOR-BOOSTED 2026-07-13 ("move those tasks up in the priority now"). Cycle-480's universal tier floor {Min:balanced, Max:deep} applies to the 72/91 profiles that declare no envelope — including scan/checklist phases (error-handling-scan, coverage-gate reporting, test-amplification) where fast-tier models handle the structured-checklist work fine per the 2026 research (caps and cheap models are quality-POSITIVE for template tasks; uniform caps only harm HARD tasks). Add explicit model_tier_envelope {Min:fast, Max:balanced} to the scan-phase profiles (an explicit envelope overrides the universal floor by design — no floor change needed) plus report-size contracts (1-2K token budgets per knowledge-base/research/token-optimization-2026 report-size-contracts finding). Parity-gate per the driver-agnostic rule: each driver's manifest must map fast to a real model. NOTE: skip phases that mechanical-scans-to-native converts to native Go (secret-leak, flake-rerun) — this item covers the scans that stay LLM-driven.
acceptance: explicit envelopes land in the scan-phase profiles; TestSpineProfilesAreDriverAgnostic stays green
report-size budgets enforced via the deliverable contract (WARN then trim guidance, not cycle-fatal)
batch soak: scan phase output tokens drop measurably in evolve tokens report; quality gates (EGPS red_count) unchanged
source: tokens report baseline 2026-07-13 (operator ultrathink); token-optimization-2026 research
